title: tetracera sarmentosa subsp. sarmentosa
intro: Tetracera sarmentosa subsp. sarmentosa, also known as the 'Vine Garlic', is a plant species that belongs to the family Dilleniaceae, characterized by its twining stems and clusters of white or yellowish flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, making it useful in treating various health issues such as wounds, infections, and fever. The health benefits of Tetracera sarmentosa subsp. sarmentosa include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in water, or by extracting the essential oils, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
